## [00:00 - 03:01]

This is a pubcast shot. I'm John Loomer. So what's the role of remarketing now? Let's go Okay, let's start from the top from the very beginning what is Remarketing remarketing is explicitly targeting people who are connected to your business Normally that would be website visitors your customer list It also includes things like engagement with your content on Facebook or Instagram So you're targeting custom audiences of these people Because you believe they're most likely to perform the action that you want the question Isn't whether it's valuable to reach these people the question is should we still explicitly target them now a quick history lesson? remarketing first became a thing way back in 2012 on Facebook That's when we were first able to target our customer list So that was a huge deal at the time because it suddenly gave us control over Who was seeing our ads and some of the most valuable people in? 2013 we were given FBX which was the ability to do website remarketing using a third-party Partner and then in 2014 Meta who was then Facebook allowed us to do it directly through the main Advertising interface targeting our website visitors. It was very valuable then it was also Very cheap then and that's probably most important So for me the majority of my strategy was some form of remarketing now my remarketing audiences were also quite large Which helped but I would do a little bit of everything right I would do general remarketing So I'd target all of my website visitors my entire email list those were engaging with my Facebook page Just because those are the people I thought were most important generally Let's focus on them to get them to perform an action I also did a lot of micro remarketing where it's like you visited this specific page You're going to get this ad it was really effective and honestly That's kind of what I was known for for quite some time and it took me some time to actually Accept the fact that maybe we shouldn't be doing that anymore. So now what is the landscape? Well, look I were the targeting much smarter now It's not perfect, but it's smarter and I think once audience segments became available That was a light bulb moment for me because then it helped us understand that remarketing is prioritized by the fault even when you're targeting broadly so it's already happening It's also much much more expensive to reach people now and that's a critical element of this So let's talk about the potential problems with remarketing first

## [03:01 - 06:01]

I think probably most importantly is that the results that come from it are misleading and often Inflated advertisers swear by remarketing because of the amazing surface level results But so many of those results come from view through conversions Where your ad was surfaced to them? They may not have seen it and they converted anyway because they're already Visiting your website or they got an email from you and they made that purchase or that conversion or whatever it is So meta takes credit because they were showing the ad before that conversion happens Additionally, it's not incremental. So these are the easiest conversions, right? It's the low-hanging fruit It's if you got a target anybody Let's target them because they're likely to perform the action that we want But we have to understand that many of these people would have converted without your ads So it may appear highly profitable on the surface, but it may not actually add much more profit Like these people would have converted anyway because they visit your website already. They get your emails already They follow you on all these various social channels and finally, it's not scalable So these are often very small audiences depending on the brand if you've got a remarketing campaign It appears to be working really well You just can't keep increasing the budget unlike going broad or anything with a larger audience to work with and part of not Scalable is you'll get amazing results for a very short period of time and then it burns out because you've reached everybody Who's likely to perform the action that you want? So what are my recommendations? Well, some would say to eliminate remarketing entirely because it happens naturally now Now I don't quite go that far if you have a reasonable budget and man There's various levels of reasonable depending on what you're trying to accomplish Especially a hundred dollars and up per day It absolutely should not be your central strategy and maybe you could even make the argument that it shouldn't be your central strategy Even if you're spending fifty dollars per day There's usually no need for general remarketing now Which again would be all the people who visit your website are on your email list etc etc Because men is already prioritizing those people. So when might you use this? I actually just used it recently for high dollar purchases and what I did was I targeted the exact people Who are also getting emails from me promoting this high dollar product in this case? I didn't want everyone to see it I don't consider everyone a prospective Customer and I really didn't want people to sign up for it who weren't a good fit So in this case I ended up with like a 27x return on ad spend but number one not scalable Number two I understand it's misleading because half of those conversions were viewed through would have happened anyway potentially All I was trying to do was have an ad to make my email more effective more than anything

## [06:01 - 07:49]
